name: Capture applications with Notion and Microsoft Teams on autopilot

parameters.message: =✅ Capture applications with Notion and Microsoft Teams on autopilot — run completed at {{ $now.toFormat('HH:mm') }}

parameters.content:
### New Email Form Submission

Serves an n8n-hosted form (From, Subject, Receivedat). Each submission becomes one execution.

Next: Download Source File

### Download Source File

GET ={{ $json.fileUrl }} — retries 3× with backoff on failure.

Ports: ①→ Parse CSV Contents · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 API key / bearer for the target API

### Parse CSV Contents

Parses the incoming CSV binary into JSON items.

Next: Clean & Reshape Rows

### Clean & Reshape Rows

Custom JavaScript: Normalize incoming email records to a stable schema

Next: Rows Pass Validation?

### Rows Pass Validation?

Two-way gate on `={{ $json.messageId }}`. TRUE continues the happy path, FALSE takes the alternate branch.

Ports: ①→ Load Rows into Notion · ②→ Quarantine Bad Rows

### Quarantine Bad Rows

Creates a new page in the Notion database — the title is pulled straight from the item's key field.

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Notion integration token

### Load Rows into Notion

Creates a new page in the Notion database — the title is pulled straight from the item's key field.

Ports: ①→ Cap Batch Volume · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Notion integration token

### Cap Batch Volume

Caps the stream at 100 items to protect downstream quotas.

Next: Save Execution Metadata

### Save Execution Metadata

Saves key fields onto the execution record — searchable later in n8n's execution list.

Next: Guard Non-Empty from

### Guard Non-Empty from

Drops items that fail the condition; only qualifying items continue (no false branch).

Next: Send Summary to Microsoft Teams

### Send Summary to Microsoft Teams

Posts a chat message into a Microsoft Teams channel — same role as a Slack notify, for teams standardized on Teams.

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Microsoft OAuth2

nodes.name: Note: Send Summary to Microsoft Teams

parameters.content:
### Capture Failure Context

Shapes the item: writes `failedNode`, `errorMessage`, `workflow`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Escalate Failure to Microsoft Teams

### Escalate Failure to Microsoft Teams

Posts a chat message into a Microsoft Teams channel — same role as a Slack notify, for teams standardized on Teams.

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Microsoft OAuth2

nodes.name: Note: Escalate Failure to Microsoft Teams

parameters.content:
# Capture applications with Notion and Microsoft Teams on autopilot

**Pattern:** Form Intake · **Level:** Core · **Trigger:** Form

## What it does
Capture applications with Notion and Microsoft Teams on autopilot. It starts from a hosted form submission.

## Why it exists
Manual drip campaigns is slow and error-prone; this workflow makes the process repeatable, monitored and hands-off, with failures surfaced instead of silently lost.

## Main flow
New Email Form Submission → Download Source File → Parse CSV Contents → Clean & Reshape Rows → Rows Pass Validation? → Load Rows into Notion → Cap Batch Volume → …

## Use it for
- Teams that want to form intake without manual effort
- Anyone automating drip campaigns in marketing
- Replacing a fragile manual process with a reliable workflow

## You get
- Keeps your systems in sync
- Scales without extra headcount
- Standardizes the process every time

## Before first run
Connect credentials: API key / bearer for the target API; Microsoft OAuth2; Notion integration token.
Follow the color key: 🟦 entry · 🟪 AI · 🟫 actions · ⬜ utilities · 🟩 outputs · 🟥 error rail.
Each node has its own doc card underneath — read those before editing.

## Try it now
Sample data is pinned on the trigger — hit **Execute Workflow** to watch it flow through every node before connecting any real credentials. Unpin the trigger (right-click → Unpin) when you're ready to go live.

## Design decisions

Bad rows are quarantined into their own store, not dropped silently and not allowed to break the whole batch — one malformed row in a 10,000-row file shouldn't stop the other 9,999 from loading, but it also shouldn't vanish without a trace.

## Practice exercises

Once it's wired up with real credentials, try:

1. Feed it a file with a few deliberately malformed rows and confirm they land in quarantine, not the main store.
2. Switch the source format (e.g. csv to xlsx) and see how little actually has to change.
3. Turn on archive mode and confirm the processed file gets repacked and stored.

## 🛟 Error handling & retries

External calls retry **3× with 2s backoff**. Anything still failing exits through its node's error output into the red error rail → context is captured and the on-call channel is alerted. Set an n8n **Error Workflow** in settings as the final safety net.

## 🛠 Customize me

Quick wins: point the data nodes at your own accounts (see 🔑 lines on each card), adjust the trigger cadence, and tune thresholds/labels to your drip campaigns process. Every node card explains its role — safe to extend at any marked branch.
